# tests/bootstrap/stage_2_smoke.hexa — accidental clobber restored

Date: 2026-05-11
By: agent (3-small-tasks batch, task C)

## What was found

The working tree had an uncommitted, unstaged modification to
`tests/bootstrap/stage_2_smoke.hexa` that **replaced the 132-line
hand-written smoke harness** (committed in 2f9789e3 — "stage 2/3 witness
harness") **with 11 lines of C-transpiler output**:

```
// Generated by HEXA self-host compiler
#include "runtime.c"

int main(int argc, char** argv) {
    hexa_set_args(argc, argv);
    fflush(stdout); fflush(stderr);
    return 0;
}
```

mtime of the clobbered file: 2026-05-11 12:19.

## Diagnosis

This is an **accidental clobber**, not an intentional simplification. A
`.hexa` test source would never contain C `#include "runtime.c"` and a
bare `int main(int argc, char** argv)` — that is the codegen *output* of
the hexa compiler. Almost certainly a `hexa build`/`cc` invocation wrote
its `-o` artifact over the source path (e.g. output path collided with
the input path), destroying the test logic.

## Action taken

`git checkout HEAD -- tests/bootstrap/stage_2_smoke.hexa` — restored the
132-line harness from HEAD (2f9789e3). No commit needed; this only
reverts an unintended working-tree change. Working tree for this file is
now clean.

If anyone *was* deliberately reworking this harness, the original intent
is not represented in the clobbered version (it has zero test logic), so
nothing of value was discarded.
